learning_outcomes.description: Recognise and analyse descriptive structures in French.
Describe the "same" object, person, place, abstract entity, emotion (etc.) in different ways to attain different goals.
Use language associated with description with a high degree of linguistic and generic accuracy.
Display critical awareness of descriptive techniques as seen in a range of texts (literary and other) in French.

Read at B2 level or above: "read with a large degree of independence, adapting style and speed of reading to different texts and purposes, and using appropriate reference sources selectively. [You should have] a broad active reading vocabulary, but may experience some difficulty with low-frequency idioms".(CEFR Illustrative Scale Overall Reading Comprehension, B2). You should be at or close to C1: " [You] can understand long and complex factual and literary texts, appreciatingdistinctions of style. [You] can understand specialised articles and longer technical instructions, even when they do not relate to [your] field." (CEFR Self-assessment grid, C1).

Use French for spoken interaction at C1 level: "express [yourself] fluently and spontaneously, almost effortlessly. [You have] a good command of a broad lexical repertoire allowing gaps to be readily overcome with circumlocutions. There is little obvious searching for expressions or avoidance strategies; only a conceptually difficult subject can hinder a natural, smooth flow of language". (CEFR C1 Overall Spoken Interaction).

Use written French at high B2/ low C1 level: You can "write clear, detailed descriptions of real or imaginary events and experiences marking the relationship between ideas in clear connected text, and following established conventions of the genre concerned" (CEFR illustrative scale for Creative Writing, B2); "Can write clear, detailed, well-structured and developed descriptions and imaginative texts in an assured, personal, natural style appropriate to the reader in mind" (CEFR illustrative scale for Creative Writing, C1).

assessments.due_date:
20/07/2026 - 5/08/2026

May be completed prior to the beginning of semester.

Students who are not able to meet the due date (due to late enrolment) must contact the Course Coordinator as soon as possible to ensure they comply with this requirement.
assessments.task_description:
Students entering FREN3115 should either have completed FREN3114 or have completed the placement questionnaire and been directed to this course by a member of the French team.

If you have completed FREN3114, no action is required on your part.

The questionnaire is here: https://courseplacements.languages-cultures.uq.edu.au/

This is a hurdle requirement. Failure to be correctly placed will result in a maximum grade of 3 in the course. 

During the Course Placement questionnaire, you may be asked to write a short text in French. This should be your own unassisted work so that we can accurately place you.
assessments.submission_guidelines: If you have completed FREN3114, no action is required.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: No assessment items will be marked until your enrolment in the course has been confirmed.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Test écrit en classe / In-class written exam
assessments.due_date:
8/09/2026

WEEK 7 - In 2026, the in-class exam will be on the TUESDAY of WEEK 7, during class.
assessments.task_description:
You receive a short descriptive text to read. You answer questions about it, which might ask you to:

  • demonstrate understanding of the text
  • explain descriptions
  • identify parts of speech (for example, relative pronouns)
  • reformulate sentences

A final question question asks you to write some additional description (120 words plus or minus 10%) which continues, or would be inserted into, the original (as indicated on the exam itself). The mark for a text which is too short will be reduced proportionally and excess material will not be marked.

This task allows you to demonstrate Reading and Writing at B2 level and gives you the opportunity to display competence at Level C on the Common European Frame of Reference (CEFR) scales.  

Duration: 70 minutes in total (60 minutes duration + 10 minutes planning time). You are allowed to start writing during the 10 minutes planning time.

Exam platform: The exam will be run in Inspera, using Safe Exam Browser (you cannot navigate online, and no-one can control your computer). There will also be paper copies of the exam available in the classroom, for those who would rather write on paper or for the unlikely situation that you encounter technical difficulties.

Exam adjustments: Please be aware that if you have an agreement re exam adjustments, you need to apply for them to be activated by the set deadline. If you do not, it might not be possible to organise your adjustment. 

Permitted materials: paper dictionary and verb tables.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: Exams submitted after the end of the submission time will incur a late penalty.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
A portfolio of short descriptive texts written over the course of the semester, totalling 6 texts and 1200 words. This assessment task is an opportunity for students to explore techniques studied in class at their own pace. While the full journal must be submitted by the set deadline, individual entries may be submitted over the course of semester for feedback, within a specified timeframe.

The portfolio must be submitted for marking twice:

By Week 5, two texts should be submitted - 15%

By Week 13, the full set must be submitted - 25%

The portfolio will be marked according to the detailed rubrics on the course website, on successful use of descriptive techniques and approaches seen in the course; variety of techniques; generic variety.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Discussion du journal de bord/ Discussion of portfolio of texts
assessments.due_date:
9/11/2026 - 20/11/2026

EXAM WEEK 1 - EXAM WEEK 2

Students will sign up at individual times for their oral interview. The student's selected timeslot will be considered the due date and time for this assessment.
assessments.task_description:
This oral relates to your journal de bord (portfolio). During this interview, you will discuss your project with the course co-ordinator and another member of French staff. You will be asked about your ideas, approach to the texts in your journal, relating them to the techniques and concepts presented in the course.

This task requires you to demonstrate competence at least at low C1 level on the CEFR scale for Spoken Interaction, in addition to your critical engagement with course content.

  • This is an individual interview.
  • The discussion should last 8-10 minutes per student
  • You speak without notes.
  • The orals will be scheduled during exam period, and will be filmed as a record of your performance.

Assessment criteria: The oral will be marked according to the detailed rubric on the course website, on: Quality of discussion; Linguistic quality (richness and accuracy); Pronunciation and Intonation

Permitted materials: This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The discussions will be conducted in person.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(27 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Weekly tutorials

The detailed plan of the course content is available on Blackboard, where you can easily consult it. Class activities typically include discussion and analysis of written or audio-visual materials, usually prepared by students before class in tandem with worksheets.
